channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Outside 9 / 12 Dorcis Avenue, 29th May
item.description:
Steve at 21 , Fell down manhole as cover missing or stolen, very bruised , also cut elbows , legs , hands and head. A neighbour helped me get out as my wife is disabled and unable to help.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dorcis Avenue, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping of mattresses and other white goods, 29th May
item.description:
Someone has left mattresses, white goods, bags of waste near the car park/next to the playground/close to the general recycling bins which is situated between Fort Road and Balaclava Road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Balaclava Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping by bowling green and park entrance, 29th May
item.description:
Looks like wardrobe doors

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queen's Mead Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Champion Park SE5, near entrance to Denmark Hill station. Dumped speakers next to WEEE receptacle., 29th May
item.description:
Please clear.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Champion Park, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Increasing signs of antisocial behaviour, 29th May
item.description:
At the end of Fulthorp Road by the barrier, there is ongoing evidence of antisocial behaviour including smart whip canisters, empty alcohol bottles and used condoms. The most recent deposit is shown in the photo, but there have been many more over the previous few weeks. It’s really disgusting given kids walk past here all the time

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Fulthorp Road, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement light in. Park is missing, 29th May
item.description:
Light set in the pavement is missing. Forms a trip hazard

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Grange Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping In Estate Bins., 29th May
item.description:
On the night of 28/05/2026, a large white van pulled up and began offloading furniture and waste into the bins belonging to the residential building. I have the license plate number as well and can send if needed. There were four gentleman.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Milton Gardens Estate, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter on Mandela way, 29th May
item.description:
There are plastic bottles, food waste and cans on the road and pavement on Mandela way. It has been there for three days.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mandela Way, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Light out, 29th May
item.description:
People may miss the round about in the dark

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mayplace Road East, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: At front garden entrance on public footpath 153 Seymour villas has an overflowing wheelie bin containing soiled bedding ,adult nappies and a disgusting assortment of plastic gloves aprons and soiled bags ripped open these are disposed of incorrectly by carers visiting 153 Seymour villas and are a serious public health issue, 29th May
item.description:
This has been happening daily children are playing on green space opposite and residents have to walk past This can not be allowed Needs some one from Bromley care to inspect along with environmental health
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 143, Seymour Villas, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car, 29th May
item.description:
The vehicle has been parked for almost 2 months and not moved. It’s stopping residents from parking

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tyrrell Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Avenue Park Road SE27 close to streetlight column, 29th May
item.description:
Avenue Park Road SE27 close to streetlight column ID number 6 (towards column 7 / side of house number 1 Maley Avenue) shopping trolley dumped on highway / footpath. Hazard. Picture attached.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Avenue Park Road, Tulse Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 204 keep mixing refuse so bins are not being collected, 29th May
item.description:
204 are not sorting refuse correctly so their bins are not being collected. This is causing major stench to neighbours and causing rats and vermin to gather and coming into neighbouring gardens. This is a public health issue especially with the current heatwave, environmental health and public safety. Please attend 204 and explain they need to use refuse bins correctly to ensure the refuse is collected as required.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 29th May
item.description:
Fly tipping left evening of 29 May, after 5pm

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Pomfret Road, Loughborough Junction, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Two matress dumped at the entrance of the woods, 29th May
item.description:
A guy with a car just happily came and dumped two matress at the entrance of the woods. I’m angry with myself for not having taken the reg!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Waterdale Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Next to one of the backyards that is on mill road, 29th May
item.description:
There’s about 3 big pot/manholes in the road and has been there for about 3+ years It’s around the end of the road but the whole road in general has potholes it’s hard for people to get through

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Charlieville Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street cleaning required, 29th May
item.description:
Street cleaners not doing their jobs. Only using a litter picker and no trolley. Leaving loads of rubbish in the street for weeks on end.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 81, Grayshott Road, Clapham Junction, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Washing machine dumped, 29th May
item.description:
Hi I saw a washing machine being taken out and dumped from a white van here on 28/5/26 I think the council have already efficiently removed it today, 29/5/26 But if it may help to report what I saw and the van’s reg no. please let me know. I live nearby, was painting the front gate of my house ar the time and saw the same van this morning.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Thorntree Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping., 29th May
item.description:
Another day, another fly tip in the usual spot.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Talbot Road, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipped large household items and white goods on grass verge/communal area., 29th May
item.description:
This is an escalating fly-tipping issue on the open grass area at the front of our block of flats, directly next to the pavement. The fridge/freezer unit and the glass table have been dumped here for at least two weeks. Because it has not been cleared, someone else has now used it as a dumping ground and added an electric cooker and more broken furniture today. The private landlord is aware of the older items, but the pile is now growing rapidly and becoming a major eyesore and environmental hazard. Please could the council enforcement team investigate this site and help get it cleared before it attracts even more bulk waste or vermin. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lanridge Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
